title: The finite DC mode commutes with subtraction

source.citation: Lloyd N. Trefethen, Spectral Methods in MATLAB, SIAM, 2000, Ch. 3, pp. 17-28.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For finite signals u and v, the DC sum of their pointwise difference equals the difference of their DC sums.
assumptions: u and v are real-valued signals
informal.expected_conclusion: The DC mode commutes with subtraction.

declarations.name: finiteDC
declarations.statement: def finiteDC {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) : Real := ∑ i ∈ s, u i

declarations.name: finiteDC_sub
declarations.statement: theorem finiteDC_sub {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u v : ι → Real) : finiteDC s (fun i => u i - v i) = finiteDC s u - finiteDC s v

tasks.input: Translate the finite-index spectral/DC identity `finiteDC_sub` into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem finiteDC_sub {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u v : ι → Real) : finiteDC s (fun i => u i - v i) = finiteDC s u - finiteDC s v

tasks.input: Identify the main finite-sum or mean-centering lemma needed for the proof.
